summary: Best pick if minimizing rack weight is the priority — it's among the lightest screws in its class and suited to fast, high-volume alpine placements. Skip it for routes with sustained hard or rock-scraped ice, where the teeth dull faster than steel alternatives.

review_links.key_takeaway: Excellent weight-to-strength ratio for alpine racks; teeth dull faster than steel on hard ice.
Aluminum tube holds up well across seasons of moderate ice but isn't the first choice for sustained hard-ice pitches.
